The area of a rectangle is calculated by multiplying the length and width. If the length is 5 cm and the width is 6 cm, what is the area of the rectangle?
A: To find the area of a rectangle, we multiply its length by its width. Given that the length is 5 cm and the width is 6 cm, we can calculate the area as follows:

\[
\text{Area} = \text{Length} \times \text{Width} = 5 \, \text{cm} \times 6 \, \text{cm} = 30 \, \text{cm}^2
\]

So, the area of the rectangle is **30 cm²**.
